race_name: We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo

top_categories.workout_context: 150 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo's belgian cobblestones and short steep bergs forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 150 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo's belgian cobblestones and short steep bergs rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
7,415ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 150 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
150 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on belgian cobblestones and short steep bergs. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,415ft of climbing on belgian cobblestones and short steep bergs does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: April in Antwerp to Oudenaarde, Belgium can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 150 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 150 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at We Ride Flanders / Ronde van Vlaanderen Cyclo demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride belgian cobblestones and short steep bergs at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 150 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 150 miles of belgian cobblestones and short steep bergs in Antwerp to Oudenaarde, Belgium.
